book_claims.claim: Halibut should be avoided due to high mercury content
book_claims.mechanism: Moderate-to-high methylmercury accumulation in a large flatfish species

book_claims.claim: Source of folate (12 mcg per 3 oz cooked, 3% DV), omega-3 (0.35–0.88 g EPA/DHA per 75 g cooked), and magnesium (24 mg per 3 oz cooked, 6% DV)
book_claims.mechanism: White fish providing omega-3, folate, and magnesium

supplementary_research.finding: Halibut has moderate mercury levels (mean 0.241 ppm); FDA/EPA categorize it as a species to eat less frequently, consistent with book's avoidance recommendation
White fish including halibut provides moderate EPA/DHA and magnesium; nutritional benefits partially offset by mercury risk at high consumption frequencies

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Moderate omega-3 content; lower than oily fish but still meaningful
Supports muscle function and glucose metabolism
High bioavailability; antioxidant and thyroid support

synergies.description: Book recommends sardines over halibut as lower-mercury oily fish alternative with higher omega-3 density
Enhances fat-soluble vitamin absorption and adds monounsaturated fats
Classic pairing; acid balances mild flavor and may improve mineral absorption
